untranslatable_reason: EEDT (Entry-Exit Decision Tool) pattern requires comparison with 'median length from Table 20.6', which is an external reference table value not available as a tradeable indicator. The rule relies on manual pattern length analysis against a static threshold rather than dynamic market-derived indicators. Pattern identification and length measurement cannot be expressed using the allowed indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, Volume, Position, Previous Bar, Returns, Bar Properties).
translation_notes: This is a meta-rule about pattern selection methodology rather than a tradeable condition. It requires: (1) identification of EEDT patterns, (2) measurement of pattern length in bars/periods, (3) comparison against Table 20.6's median value. None of these can be operationalized with the available indicators. Exit conditions marked as AUTHOR_SILENT with no guidance. Direction is context-dependent, indicating direction should be determined by breakout direction of the specific pattern, which varies case-by-case.
